zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Saye於1325年出生在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯時，她的父親Geoffrey De Saye II20歲，母親Maud de Beauchamp11歲。她於1351年11月12日與William Fiennes I在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1378年6月29日在英國素可賀斯特蒙瑟享年53歲，埋葬在英國素可賀斯特蒙瑟。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Saye于1325年出生在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯时，她的父亲Geoffrey De Saye II20岁，母亲Maud de Beauchamp11岁。她于1351年11月12日与William Fiennes I在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1378年6月29日在英国素可賀斯特蒙瑟享年53岁，埋葬在英国素可賀斯特蒙瑟。

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Saye was born in 1325, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Geoffrey De Saye II, was 20 and her mother, Maud de Beauchamp, was 11. She married William Fiennes I on 12 November 1351, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 29 June 1378, in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England, at the age of 53, and was buried in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Saye est née en 1325, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Geoffrey De Saye II, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Maud de Beauchamp, avait 11 ans. Elle a épousé William Fiennes I le 12 novembre 1351, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 29 juin 1378, à Herstmonceux, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterrée à Herstmonceux, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Saye 1325 in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Geoffrey De Saye II 20 und ihre Mutter Maud de Beauchamp 11. Sie hat am 12. November 1351 in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England William Fiennes I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 29. Juni 1378 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England und wurde in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Saye è nata il nel 1325 nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Geoffrey De Saye II, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Maud de Beauchamp, aveva 11 anni. Lei sposato William Fiennes I il 12 novembre 1351, nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 29 giugno 1378, nel Herstmonceux, Sussex, England, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolta a Herstmonceux, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Sayeが1325年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで生まれた時，父Geoffrey De Saye IIは20歳，母Maud de Beauchampは11歳であった。1351年11月12日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスでWilliam Fiennes Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1378年6月29日にイギリススセックスハーストモンセウックスで53歳で死去し，イギリススセックスハーストモンセウックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Saye(이)가 1325년 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Geoffrey De Saye II(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Beauchamp(은)는 11세였다. 그녀는 1351년 11월 12일 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 William Fiennes I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1378년 6월 29일 영국 수세스 허스트몬스우스에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 허스트몬스우스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Saye nasceu em 1325, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Geoffrey De Saye II, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Beauchamp, tinha 11 anos. Ela casou-se com William Fiennes I em 12 de novembro de 1351, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 29 de junho de 1378, em Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 53 anos, e foi sepultada em Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Saye родилась в 1325 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Geoffrey De Saye II было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Beauchamp, 11. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Fiennes I 12 ноября 1351 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 29 июня 1378 г. в местности хирстмонсеукс, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 53 лет и была похоронена в местности хирстмонсеукс, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Saye nació en 1325, en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Geoffrey De Saye II, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Maud de Beauchamp, tenía 11 años. Se casó con William Fiennes I el 12 de noviembre de 1351, en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 29 de junio de 1378, en Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultada en Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra.
